flashcards.front: What is the mathematical formula for hit-or-miss transform?
What are the two structuring elements used in HMT and what do they represent?
flashcards.back: B₁ (foreground/hit element): pattern that must be present; B₂ (background/miss element): pattern that must be absent

flashcards.front: Name three applications of hit-or-miss transform
flashcards.back: Pattern recognition, feature detection (corners, endpoints), thinning/skeletonization, pruning

flashcards.front: What is the main limitation of hit-or-miss transform?
flashcards.back: Sensitivity to noise and requiring exact pattern matching

flashcards.front: How does HMT differ from basic erosion operation?
flashcards.back: HMT uses two structuring elements and considers both foreground and background patterns, while erosion uses one element and only considers foreground
